_comment: Paper-acquisition manifest. Seeded 2026-07-22 by the fallback-chain probe (7 papers: 5 c19 pilot + 2 stress cases). Workflow 1 (acquire) owns and extends this file corpus-wide; Workflow 2 (analyze) reads it and must treat quality grades as coverage contracts. verbatim=false means the file preserves facts/structure but not the authors' prose and should be re-extracted before quote-level verification.

papers.route: Route 4 (e-print LaTeX source): curl -sL https://arxiv.org/e-print/2407.01892, gzip/tar extracted, main file sample.tex (649 lines) plus sample.bbl (178 lines, fully typeset bibliography, 34 entries)
papers.notes: REDO of a prior non-verbatim synthesis file, now overwritten with true verbatim extraction. Route taken: went straight to Route 4 (e-print) per task instructions since prior probe already confirmed HTML/WebFetch routes return only AI summaries. curl -sL https://arxiv.org/e-print/2407.01892 returned a gzip tarball (~5.9MB); extracted to find sample.tex (main LaTeX, 649 lines), sample.bbl (formatted bibliography, 34 \bibitem entries), sample.bib (raw BibTeX superset with uncited extras), and 12 image files (benchmark_construction.png, results.png/result.png, supp1.png, supp2.png, temp1-7.png) which could not be transcribed as text (captions and surrounding prose preserved instead, explicitly marked as image-only in file). Body text fully de-LaTeXed: stripped \cite{}/\ref{}/\textit/\textbf/\hl formatting commands while preserving all authorial sentences verbatim, including Abstract, unn-lab

papers.authors: Sönke Tenckhoff, Mario Koddenbrock, Erik Rodner (KI-Werkstatt / FB2, University of Applied Sciences Berlin / HTW Berlin)

papers.notes: IDENTITY VERIFIED: arXiv id 2602.14743 resolves to a real, very recently posted paper: "LLMStructBench: Benchmarking Large Language Model Structured Data Extraction" by Sönke Tenckhoff, Mario Koddenbrock, and Erik Rodner (KI-Werkstatt / FB2, HTW Berlin), submitted 16 Feb 2026 (v1, only version), cs.CL cross-listed cs.LG, no venue/journal-ref in comments (appears to be an arXiv-only preprint at fetch time). This is 2024+ (in fact 2026) and squarely on-topic for the benchmark-design project: it introduces a 995-example, 5-use-case benchmark for evaluating 22 open-weight LLMs (Qwen3, Phi, Gemma3, Deepseek-R1, Llama3 families) plus GPT-4o on natural-language-to-JSON structured extraction, across 5 prompting strategies (J/P/PJ/J+/PJ+), with a novel micro-averaged F1 metric (fuzzy/coercible value credit) and a document-level validity (DOC_micro) metric, finding prompting strategy and model-fam

One genuine extraction gap: Table 12 (Mixed Constraints per-level results) caption was captured but cell data was not present in the HTML->text dump at that location (likely a parser artifact at a table boundary) — flagged explicitly inline in the file rather than filled in. Primary body route (Step 2, curl + BeautifulSoup on arxiv.org/html/2502.09933) succeeded immediately with a full LaTeXML render (v5, ~540KB) — no need to fall back to ar5iv, LaTeX source, or PDF. First-pass extraction script had a bug where nested multi-panel figures (ltx_figure containing ltx_figure_panel sub-figures, e.g. Figure 2, 4, 5, 6, 11) only captured the first sub-panel's image+caption and skipped the rest plus the outer figure's final caption; this was caught and fixed by rewriting the figure-handling logic to recursively walk div wrappers (ltx_flex_figure/ltx_flex_cell) and process every nested figure panel in order — re-diffed old vs new extraction to confirm the fix only ADDED missing figure/table captions (strict superset, no content lost)
